sentences.text: Varicose Veins Number: 0050 Applicable CPT / HCPCS / ICD-10 Codes Scope of Policy This Clinical Policy Bulletin addresses treatment of varicose veins.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: I. Medically Necessary A. Aetna considers the following procedures medically necessary for treatment of varicose veins: 1.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Great saphenous vein or small saphenous vein ligation / division / stripping; 2.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Radiofrequency endovenous occlusion (VNUS procedure); and 3.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Endovenous laser ablation of the saphenous vein (ELAS) (also known as endovenous laser treatment (EVLT)) When the following criteria are met: 1.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Incompetence at the saphenofemoral junction or saphenopopliteal junction is documented by recent (performed within the past 6 months) Doppler or duplex ultrasound scanning, and all of the following criteria are met: a. Ultrasound documented junctional reflux duration of 500 milliseconds (ms) or greater in the saphenofemoral or saphenopopliteal vein to be treated; and b. Vein size is 4.5 mm or greater in diameter measured by ultrasound below the saphenofemoral or saphenopopliteal junction (not valve diameter at junction); and c. Saphenous varicosities result in any of the following: i. Intractable ulceration secondary to venous stasis; or ii.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: More than 1 episode of minor hemorrhage from a ruptured superficial varicosity; or a single significant hemorrhage from a ruptured superficial varicosity, especially if transfusion of blood is required; or iii.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Saphenous varicosities result in either of the following, and symptoms persist despite a 3-month trial of conservative managementFootnote1* (including medical grade (20 mmHg or greater) gradient support compression stockings).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: a. Recurrent superficial thrombophlebitis; or b. Severe and persistent pain and swelling interfering with activities of daily living. 2.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Surgical ligation (including subfascial endoscopic perforator vein surgery (SEPS)) or endovenous ablation procedures are considered medically necessary for the treatment of incompetent perforating veins when all of the following are met: a. Perforating vein diameter measured by recent ultrasound of 3.5 mm or greater; and b. Outward flow duration of 500 msec duration or more; and c. Perforating vein is located underneath an active or healed venous stasis ulcer (also known as CEAP C5 or C6). See Appendix. 3.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Endovenous ablation procedures are considered medically necessary adjunctive treatment of symptomatic accessory saphenous veins a. who are being treated or have previously ;been treated by one of the procedures listed above for incompetence (i.e., reflux) at the saphenofemoral junction or saphenopopliteal junction; and b. anatomically related persistent junctional reflux is demonstrated after the great or small saphenous veins have been removed or ablated.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Note: Initially, endovenous ablation therapy of the first vein and of the second and subsequent veins in each affected extremity is considered medically necessary when criteria are met.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Note: Thus one primary code and one secondary code for each affected leg are considered medically necessary for initial endovenous ablation treatment.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Note: Additional endovenous ablation therapy is considered medically necessary for persons with persistent or recurrent junctional reflux of the greater saphenous vein, lesser saphenous vein following initial endovenous ablation therapy.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In order to authorize additional endovenous ablation, there should be documentation that the member continues to have symptoms and ultrasound showing persistent junctional reflux.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Additional endovenous ablation therapy may also be necessary for treatment of accessory saphenous veins as noted above.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: These procedures are considered experimental, investigational, or unproven for treatment of varicose tributaries and accessory veins other than the accessory saphenous vein.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: These procedures are considered cosmetic for all other indications.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Note: Doppler or duplex ultrasound studies are considered necessary prior to varicose vein treatment to assess the anatomy and to determine whether there is significant reflux at the saphenofemoral or saphenopopliteal junction requiring surgical repair, and after completion of the treatment to determine the success of the procedure and detect thrombosis.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Ultrasound guidance is inclusive of the VNUS or ELAS procedures.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Note: The term endovenous catheter ablation (EVCA) is a non-specific term that refers to the several catheter based minimally invasive alternatives to surgical stripping such as radiofrequency endovenous occlusion (VNUS procedure) and endovenous laser ablation of the saphenous vein (ELAS).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: In assessing the medical necessity of EVCA, reference should be made to the specific technique that is being employed.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: B. Aetna considers liquid or foam sclerotherapy (endovenous chemical ablation) (e.g., Varithena) medically necessary treatment of symptomatic saphenous veins, varicose tributaries, accessory, and perforator veins when all of the following criteria are met: 1.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Vein size is 2.5 mm or greater in diameter, measured by recent ultrasound; and 2.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Varicosities result in any of the following: a. Intractable ulceration secondary to venous stasis; or b. More than 1 episode of minor hemorrhage from a ruptured superficial varicosity; or a single significant hemorrhage from a ruptured superficial varicosity, especially if transfusion of blood is required; or c. Varicosities result in either of the following, and symptoms persist despite a 3- month trial of conservative managementFootnote1* (including medical grade (20 mmHg or greater) gradient support compression stockings): i. Recurrent superficial thrombophlebitis; or ii.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Severe and persistent pain and swelling interfering with activities of daily living; and 3.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: If member has incompetence (i.e., reflux) at the saphenofemoral junction, the junctional reflux is being treated by one or more of the endovenous ablation or ligation and division procedures noted in section A above to reduce the risk of varicose vein recurrence.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Note: Varithena has not been proven to be more effective than other methods of foam sclerotherapy.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Note: Since ultrasound-monitored or duplex-guided techniques for sclerotherapy have not been shown to definitively increase the effectiveness or safety of this procedure, these tests are only considered medically necessary when initially performed to determine the extent and configuration of varicose veins.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Note: Ultrasound- or radiologically guided or monitoring techniques are of no proven value when performed solely to guide the needle or introduce the sclerosant into the varicose veins.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Note: The number of medically necessary sclerotherapy injection sessions varies with the number of anatomical areas that have to be injected, as well as the response to each injection.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Usually 1 to 3 injections are necessary to obliterate any vessel, and 10 to 40 vessels, or a set of up to 20 injections in each leg, may be treated during one treatment session.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Initially, up to two sets of injections of sclerosing solution in multiple veins in each affected leg (i.e., a total of four sets of injections if both legs are affected) are considered medically necessary when criteria are met.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Note: A set of injections is defined as multiple sclerotherapy injections during a treatment session.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Additional sets of injections of sclerosing solution are considered medically necessary for persons with persistent or recurrent symptoms.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: C. Aetna considers ambulatory phlebectomy or transilluminated powered phlebectomy (TriVex System) medically necessary adjunctive treatment of symptomatic saphenous veins, varicose tributaries, accessory, and perforator veins when all of the following criteria are met: 1.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Vein size is 2.5 mm or greater in diameter; and 2.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Varicosities result in any of the following: a. Intractable ulceration secondary to venous stasis; or b. More than 1 episode of minor hemorrhage from a ruptured superficial varicosity; or a single significant hemorrhage from a ruptured superficial varicosity, especially if transfusion of blood is required; or c. Varicosities result in either of the following, and symptoms persist despite a 3- month trial of conservative managementFootnote1* (including medical grade (20 mmHg or greater) gradient support compression stockings): i. Recurrent superficial thrombophlebitis; or ii.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Severe and persistent pain and swelling interfering with activities of daily living; and 3.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: If member has incompetence (i.e., reflux) at the saphenofemoral junction or saphenopopliteal junction, the junctional reflux is being treated by one or more of the procedures noted in section A above to reduce the risk of varicose vein recurrence.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Note: Initially, up to two multiple stab phlebectomy incisions in each affected extremity (i.e., a total of four multiple stab incisions if both legs are affected) are considered medically necessary when criteria are met.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Additional multiple stab phlebectomy incisions are considered medically necessary for persons with persistent or recurrent symptoms.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Note: A set of stab phlebectomy incisions is defined as multiple stab phlebectomy incisions during a treatment session.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Footnote1*Note: A trial of conservative management is not required for persons with persistent or recurrent varicosities who have undergone prior endovenous catheter ablation procedures or stripping/division/ligation in the same leg because conservative management is unlikely to be successful in this situation.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: D. Aetna considers valvular reconstruction medically necessary for chronic venous insufficiency. II.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Experimental, Investigational, or Unproven A. The following procedures are considered experimental, investigational, or unproven because they have not been proven in direct comparative studies to be as effective as established alternatives or has not been proven for the listed indications: 1.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Ambulatory phlebectomy or transilluminated powered phlebectomy for treatment of junctional reflux; 2.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Ambulatory selective variceal ablation under local anesthetic (the ASVAL procedure) for the treatment of symptomatic great saphenous vein; 3.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Endomechanical or mechanochemical ablation (MOCA) (e.g., ClariVein); 4.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Endovenous ablation (laser or radiofrequency) for the treatment of reflux of the common femoral vein; 5.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: External valvuloplasty for the treatment of symptomatic great saphenous vein; 6.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Measurements of plasma growth factors (e.g., angiopoietin-1 (ANG1), angiopoietin-2 (ANG2), epidermal growth factor (EGF), platelet-derived growth factor (PDGF), and vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF)) for predicting adequacy of treatment and the possibility of recurrence of varicose veins before and after treatment with endovenous laser ablation; 7.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Micronized purified flavonoid fraction; 8.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Peri-procedural thromboprophylaxis (including pharmacotherapy) for the prevention of deep vein thrombosis in varicose vein interventions; 9.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Polymorphism genotyping of matrix metalloproteinases genes (e.g., MMP1, MMP2, MMP3, and MMP7) as markers of predisposition to varicose veins; 10.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Sclerotherapy for treatment of reflux of iliac veins, the saphenofemoral junction, or saphenopopliteal junction; sclerotherapy alone has not been shown to be effective for persons with reflux at the saphenofemoral or saphenopopliteal junctions; under established guidelines, individuals with reflux should also be treated with endovenous ablation, ligation or division of the junction to reduce the risk of varicose vein recurrence; Note: Gel sclerotherapy of ovarian veins with the internal iliac veins, is considered medically necessary for the treatment of pelvic congestion syndrome - see CPB 0441 - Pelvic Congestion Syndrome: Treatments; 11.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Synthetic matrix metalloproteinases inhibitors; 12.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Transdermal laser treatment of large varicose veins; Note: Although transdermal Nd:YAG laser has been shown to be effective for the treatment of telangiectasias and reticular veins, treatment of these small veins is considered cosmetic; 13.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Use of medical adhesive (also referred to as cyanoacrylate superglue, n-butyl- cyanoacrylate) (e.g., VariClose Vein Sealing System, VenaSeal Closure System) for the treatment of varicose veins; 14. VeinGogh Ohmic Thermolysis System; 15.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: VeinOPlus vascular device for the treatment of muscle atrophy due to varicose veins.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: B. Endovenous ablation procedures are considered experimental, investigational, or unproven for treatment of varicose tributaries and accessory veins other than the accessory saphenous vein. III.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Cosmetic A. The following procedures are considered cosmetic because they are used to treat small veins that do not cause pain bleeding, ulceration, or other medical problems: 1.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Ambulatory phlebectomy and the TriVex system for veins less than 2.5 mm in diameter and all other indications; Note: Transilluminated powered phlebectomy has not been proven to be superior to other methods of varicose vein removal; therefore, the TriVex procedure should be billed as any other varicose vein removal procedure; 2.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Asclera polidocanol injection; Note: Although Asclera has been approved by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for the treatment of telangiectasias and reticular veins less than 3 mm in diameter, treatment of these small veins is considered cosmetic; 3.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Photothermal sclerosis (also referred to as an intense pulsed light source, e.g., the PhotoDerm VascuLight, VeinLase), used to treat small veins such as small varicose veins and spider veins; 4.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Sclerotherapy for treatment of veins less than 2.5 mm in diameter and for all other indications.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: B. Endovenous ablation procedures are considered cosmetic for all other indications than listed in Policy section I. IV.
sentences.label: CLAIM

thrombophlebitis in the puerperium O87.4 Varicose veins of lower extremity in the puerperium O87.9 Venous complication in the puerperium, unspecified Varicose veins are a common condition.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: In adult western populations visible varicose veins are present in 20 to 25 % of women and 10 to 15 % of men.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In most persons, varicose veins do not cause symptoms other than poor cosmesis.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Varicose vein surgery is one of the most commonly performed cosmetic procedures in the United States.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Most varicose veins do not require medical treatment (Tapley et al, 2003).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In some cases, however, the circulation may be hindered enough to cause swelling of the foot and ankle, discomfort, a tingling sensation, or a feeling of heaviness.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: For most people with varicose veins, wearing specially fitted elastic stockings is all that is needed.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The stockings should be carefully fitted to the individual, providing the most pressure in the lowest part of the leg.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The stockings should be put on when first arising in the morning, preferably before getting out of bed.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Exercise such as walking or cycling also helps promote better circulation from the lower part of the body.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Resting with the legs elevated will help promote circulation; in contrast, sitting with the legs crossed can aggravate the condition.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Authorities have recommended 6 or more months as a reasonable duration for a trial conservative management (NHS, 2005).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: A substantial proportion of varicose vein symptoms respond to conservative management.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: A randomized controlled clinical trial compared surgery (n = 124) to conservative management (n = 122) of varicose veins (Michaels et al, 2006).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Conservative management consisted of lifestyle advice relating to exercise, leg elevation, management of weight and diet, and the use of compression hosiery.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In the surgical arm of the trial patients received the same lifestyle advice but also underwent surgical treatment, consisting of flush ligation of sites of reflux, stripping of the long saphenous vein and multiple phlebectomies, as appropriate.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Although a greater proportion of patients assigned to surgery plus lifestyle advice at relieving symptoms at 1 year, approximately one-third of subjects assigned to conservative management reported some relief from conservative management with compression hosiery.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: At 2 years, there was no significant difference in symptom improvement between groups assigned to conservative management versus surgery.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors posited that the lack of significant difference in symptomatology between groups at 2 years may have been due to cross-overs, with 7 patients in the conservative management group opting for surgery in year 1 and 37 patients opting for surgery in year 2.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The study also found that persons assigned to surgery plus lifestyle advice had a greater improvement in cosmesis and quality of life than persons assigned to lifestyle advice alone, although it is not known whether improvements in quality of life were primarily related to improvements in cosmesis versus reductions in symptomatology.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Weaknesses of the study included a substantial loss to follow-up in all groups.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Fifteen of the 124 patients assigned to surgery either refused surgery in favor of conservative management or declined surgery due to fitness.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Of the remaining 109 patients who underwent surgery, 43 were lost to follow up by the first year.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Of subjects assigned to conservative treatment, 21 were lost to follow-up by the first year.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors observed that, although surgery was more effective at improving symptomatology at 1 year, a substantial proportion of patients assigned to conservative treatment reported resolution or improvements in aching (26 %), heaviness (46 %), itching (56 %), and swelling (68 %).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In addition, a substantial proportion of persons assigned to conservative management reported improvements in cosmesis. "
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Indeed, 22 % of the latter reported that they no longer had cosmetic concerns.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These observations suggest a substantial benefit from surgery but perhaps support the case for careful evaluation of patients’ symptoms and problems when considering surgical treatment."
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: An editorialist noted that the short follow-up of subjects assigned to surgery may result in an underestimate of the costs and an exaggeration of the benefits of surgery (van Rij, 2006).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: By the third year, only 40 % of subjects in the study by Michaels et al assigned to surgery were assessed.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The editorialist noted, however, that most recurrences are diagnosed later than 3 years.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Focusing on the short-term may lead to an under- estimate of cost and an over-estimate of benefit.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The editorialist stated that prospective comparisons of durability up to 5 years and longer are infrequent and yet by this time the recurrence rate may be as high as 50 %.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In patients with varicose veins, leg pain may be associated with superficial thrombophlebitis or venous leg ulcers.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In evaluating the role of varicose vein surgery in treatment of these conditions, the effectiveness of varicose vein surgery must be compared to conservative management.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: If the patient is suffering from superficial thrombophlebitis, conservative management is indicated.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: According to available guidelines, uncomplicated superficial thrombophlebitis is usually treated symptomatically with heat, simple analgesia, non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), and compression stockings (SCHIN, 2002).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Treatment should continue until symptoms have completely subsided (usually 2 to 6 weeks to subside but the thrombosed vein may be palpable and tender for months).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: More severe thrombophlebitis, as indicated by the degree of pain and redness and the extent of abnormality, should be treated by bed rest with elevation of the extremity and application of hot, wet compresses.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Leg ulcers arising from venous problems are called venous (varicose or stasis) ulcers.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The main conservative treatment has been to apply a firm compression garment (bandage or stocking) to the lower leg in order to help the blood return back up the leg.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Cullum et al (2002) conducted a meta-analysis of the literature on the effectiveness of compression bandaging and stockings in the treatment of varicose leg ulcers.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that compression increases ulcer-healing rates compared with no compression.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The authors also found that multi-layered systems are more effective than single-layered systems.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: High compression is more effective than low compression but there are no clear differences in the effectiveness of different types of high compression.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In a meta-analysis, Nelson et al (2002) found circumstantial evidence of the benefit of compression in reducing recurrence of varicose ulcers.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors also noted that recurrence rates may be lower in high compression hosiery than in medium compression hosiery and therefore patients should be offered the strongest compression with which they can comply.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In a trial conducted at 20 centers in the United Kingdom, funded by the National Institute for Health Research Health Technology Assessment Program, Gohel, et al. (
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: 2019) randomly assigned 450 patients with venous leg ulcers to receive compression therapy and undergo early endovenous ablation of superficial venous reflux within 2 weeks after randomization (early-intervention group) or to receive compression therapy alone, with consideration of endovenous ablation deferred until after the ulcer was healed or until 6 months after randomization if the ulcer was unhealed (deferred-intervention group).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The primary outcome was the time to ulcer healing.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Secondary outcomes were the rate of ulcer healing at 24 weeks, the rate of ulcer recurrence, the length of time free from ulcers (ulcer-free time) during the first year after randomization, and patient-reported health-related quality of life.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Patient and clinical characteristics at baseline were similar in the two treatment groups.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The time to ulcer healing was shorter in the early- intervention group than in the deferred-intervention group; more patients had healed ulcers with early intervention (hazard ratio for ulcer healing, 1.38; 95% confidence interval [CI], 1.13 to 1.68; P=0.001).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The median time to ulcer healing was 56 days (95% CI, 49 to 66) in the early-intervention group and 82 days (95% CI, 69 to 92) in the deferred-intervention group.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The rate of ulcer healing at 24 weeks was 85.6% in the early-intervention group and 76.3% in the deferred-intervention group.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The median ulcer-free time during the first year after trial enrollment was 306 days (interquartile range, 240 to 328) in the early-intervention group and 278 days (interquartile range, 175 to 324) in the deferred-intervention group (P=0.002).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The most common procedural complications of endovenous ablation were pain and deep-vein thrombosis.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Early endovenous ablation of superficial venous reflux resulted in faster healing of venous leg ulcers and more time free from ulcers than deferred endovenous ablation. A review (Weiß, et al.,
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: 2019) noted that weak points of the study by Gohel, et al.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: are I. the relatively small (2–3 cm 2 ) ulcers, which are not representative of the ulcers commonly seen in inpatient/outpatient wound facilities, II.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: the ulcer duration being very short (~3 months) compared with real‐life situations, where patients present with long‐standing ulcers, III.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: the study population being almost exclusively white, and IV.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: venous ulcers are included exclusively, whereas many ulcers are due to a combination of venous and arterial problems.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: 2 I. the relatively small (2–3 cm ) ulcers, which are not representative of the ulcers commonly seen in inpatient/outpatient wound facilities, II.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: the ulcer duration being very short (~3 months) compared with real‐life situations, where patients present with long‐standing ulcers, III.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: the study population being almost exclusively white, and IV.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: venous ulcers are included exclusively, whereas many ulcers are due to a combination of venous and arterial problems.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The reviewers concluded: "Adequately powered, high‐quality randomized controlled trials comparing the three available treatment options – compression, conventional venous surgery and endovenous treatment – are still required.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: These should measure and report outcomes that include time to ulcer healing, ulcer recurrence, adverse events, quality of life and cost‐effectiveness."
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Adequately powered, high‐quality randomized controlled trials comparing the three available treatment options – compression, conventional venous surgery and endovenous treatment – are still required.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: These should measure and report outcomes that include time to ulcer healing, ulcer recurrence, adverse events, quality of life and cost‐effectiveness.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: According to a systematic review of the evidence, pentoxifilline has also been shown to be effective for treatment of venous leg ulcers (Nelson et al, 2002).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: According to the systematic evidence review, compression has been shown to prevent venous leg ulcers.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The effectiveness of vein surgery for prevention or treatment of venous ulcers is "unknown" (Nelson et al, 2002).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Beyond conservative therapy, the treatment of varicose veins in the lower legs includes injection/compression sclerotherapy and surgical stripping or ligation or a combination of these approaches depending upon the severity of the condition.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Despite many years of experience, there is still a disappointingly high recurrence rate of varices because many patients are inadequately investigated before treatment.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: As it has been shown that physical examination alone is unreliable, pre-treatment Doppler or Duplex ultrasound examination must be performed for localization of the sites of incompetence to allow the individualization of the treatment strategy for each patient.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Photographs or office diagrams may be helpful in assessing the size and extent of the varices.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Under established guidelines, the basic tenet of successful treatment is to eliminate the primary and secondary sources of the reflux.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: These sources are usually a nearby perforator, or most often a major junction that causes redirected venous return through veins with intact valves.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Sclerotherapy has been found to be more effective in patients with dilated superficial or residual varicose veins, recurrent varicosities or incompetent perforating veins of small to moderate size (less than 6 mm) without vein reflux.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Large varicosities do not respond as well as small varicosities to sclerotherapy (Rosenberg, 2006; MSAC, 2011; MAS, 2011).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Inadvertent intra-arterial injection has been an untoward sequela of sclerotherapy.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Almost all cases of painful varicosities are associated with junctional reflux.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: When reflux at the saphenofemoral and/or saphenopopliteal junctions is present, accepted guidelines provide that sclerotherapy should not be performed until surgical ligation and division of the junction has been done.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The junctions themselves can not be adequately treated by sclerotherapy as junctional reflux must be addressed by endovenous ablation methods or surgical ligation or stripping (Jakobsen, 1979; MSAC, 2008; MSAC, 2011).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Although varicosities can occasionally be present in the absence or reflux, there is a lack of evidence from reliable clinical studies of the effectiveness of sclerotherapy in relieving symptomatic varicosities not associated with junctional reflux.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The sole randomized controlled clinical trial (n = 25) to address the efficacy of sclerotherapy in varicosities not associated with junctional reflux (Kalhe and Leng, 2004) evaluated sclerotherapy efficacy in obliterating varicosities, but did not address its effectiveness at relieving pain.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Although sclerotherapy can be used to treat visible subcuticular veins (i.e., spider angiomas, and telangiectasias) less than 2.5 mm in size, these small veins do not cause symptoms and their treatment is purely cosmetic (MSAC, 2011).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Doppler ultrasound is often used in conjunction with other non-invasive physiologic testing to characterize the anatomy and physiology of the varicose vein network prior to injection or surgical intervention.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: However, duplex scans are also sometimes utilized during the sclerotherapy procedure itself.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Their purported usefulness in this regard includes the localization of deep or inaccessible injection sites, such as when there are extensive networks of large deep varicosities, areas of significant reflux between superficial and deep systems, or risks to arterial structures.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Ultrasound has also been used to monitor the effectiveness of compressive sclerotherapy in obliterating the lumen of the target vein and reducing reflux/retrograde flow.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: However, these indications have not been scientifically validated.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: There is little evidence, in the form of randomized prospective clinical trials, to support that ultrasound makes a significant difference in optimizing outcome or decreasing complications, from sclerotherapy for varicose veins, when compared to non- ultrasound-guided techniques.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: A structured evidence review conducted by the Alberta Heritage Foundation for Medical Research (AHFMR) (2003) concluded that “the reviewed evidence does not adequately address the questions; which sclerosant is superior and which technique with or without ultrasound guidance is most efficacious.”
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Venous reflux can be elicited manually by calf muscle compression and release, by the Valsava maneuver, or by pneumatic tourniquet release (Markovic & Shortell, 2014).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: If saphenofemoral reflux lasting longer than 500 ms is present, the diameter of the great saphenous veins (GSV) is recorded 2.5 cm distal to the saphenofemoral junction.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The compliant GSV adjusts its luminal size to the level of transmural pressure, and measurement of its diameter has been shown to reflect the severity of hemodynamic compromise in limbs with GSV reflux. In a cohort study, Navarro, et al. (
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: 2002) evaluated the relationship of GSV diameter determined in the thigh and calf to clinical severity of reflux in 112 legs in 85 consecutive patients with saphenofemoral junction and truncal GSV incompetence.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The authors stated that they found that the GSV diameter proved to be a relatively accurate measure of hemodynamic impairment and clinical severity in a model of saphenofemoral junction and GSV incompete ce, predicting not only the absence of abnormal reflux, but also the presence of critical venous incompetence.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: A GSV diameter of 5.5 mm or less predicted the absence of abnormal reflux, with a sensitivity of 78 %, a specificity of 87 %, positive and negative predictive values of 78 %, and an accuracy of 82 %.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Ligation and division of the saphenofemoral and/or saphenopopliteal junction is indicated in patients with symptomatic varicose veins who have failed conservative management, when reflux of greater than 0.5 seconds is demonstrated by Doppler examination or Duplex scanning.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The literature states that operative excision of varicose veins in the leg(s) should be reserved for those that are very large (greater than 6 mm), extensive in distribution, or occur in large clusters.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Ligation alone usually results in a high recurrence rate of the varicose vein, which may then require sclerotherapy treatment (MSAC, 2008).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Stripping of the greater and/or lesser saphenous vein, performed in conjunction with ligation and division of their respective junctions, is indicated when the saphenous veins themselves show varicose changes (usually greater than 1 cm in diameter).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Varicose vein surgery and/or sclerotherapy during pregnancy is not appropriate because dilatation of veins in the legs is physiologic and will revert to normal after delivery, at which time a more accurate appraisal can be made.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Visible subcuticular veins (i.e., spider angiomas, and telangiectasias) less than 2.5 mm in size do not cause symptoms and their treatment is purely cosmetic.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Ambulatory phlebectomy (AP) (also known as microphlebectomy) is a minimally invasive procedure performed under local anesthesia, and is an accepted outpatient therapy for the removal of varicose veins.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: This treatment allows excision of almost all of the large varicose veins except the proximal long saphenous vein, which is better-managed by stripping.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Non-refluxing varicose veins on the surface of the leg, not including the saphenous veins, may be treated as an outpatient procedure under local anaesthetic using ambulatory phlebectomy (MSAC, 2011).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: However, recurrence rates can be high if the source of the reflux is not treated (MSAC, 2011).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The junctions themselves can not be treated with simple phlebectomy as junctional reflux must be addressed by endovenous ablation methods or rarely by surgical ligation and stripping (MSAC, 2011; Weiss, 2007).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Patients can ambulate immediately after AP.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Complications associated with AP include blister formation, localized thrombophlebitis, skin necrosis, hemorrhage, and persistent edema.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The use of broad compression pads following AP reduces hemorrhage and enhances resorption.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The TriVex System (transilluminated powered phlebectomy) is an alternative method of providing ambulatory phlebectomy.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: This entails endoscopic resection and ablation of the superficial veins using an illuminator and a "powered vein rejector", a small powered surgical device.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: In this procedure, veins are marked with a magic marker.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: In order to enhance visualization of the veins, a bright light is introduced into the leg through a tiny incision.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The powered vein rejector, which has a powered oscillating end, is then introduced to cut and dislodge the veins.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The pieces of vein are then gently retrieved by suction down a tube.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Transilluminated powered phlebectomy is usually performed in the hospital on an outpatient basis and under general anesthesia or using local anesthesia with sedation.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The manufacturer of the TriVex System states that the unique illumination feature allows the surgeon to quickly and accurately target and remove the vein and then visually confirm its complete extraction.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The manufacturer claims that this new process makes varicose vein removal more effective, complete and less traumatic for patients, by reducing the number of incisions required to perform the procedure and the duration of surgery.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The manufacturer also claims that this method not only reduces the pain associated with varicose vein removal but also reduces the potential for post-operative infection.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: There is inadequate evidence, however, in the published peer-reviewed medical literature substantiating these claims.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The potential advantages of the TriVex System over standard ambulatory phlebectomy have not been proven.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Therefore, the TriVex procedure should be billed as any other varicose vein removal procedure.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The term endovenous catheter ablation (EVCA) has been used to refer to the several new catheter based minimally invasive alternatives to surgical stripping, including laser ablation and radiofrequency ablation.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Endovenous catheter ablation and surgical ligation/stripping are indicated for treatment of the same general population: patients in whom the great and/or small saphenous veins have reflux or incompetence of 0.5 seconds or longer demonstrated on duplex scanning, and varicose vein symptoms significantly impinge on quality of life (MSAC, 2011).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: These patients have exhausted conservative treatment measures, and sclerotherapy is considered unlikely to provide successful results.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Endovenous laser ablation and radiofrequency ablation are essentially identical except for the use of different specialized equipment and catheters, with thermal energy delivered through either a radiofrequency catheter or laser fiber (MSAC, 2011).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The objectives of the two treatments are the same, being the destruction or ablation of a refluxing vein or segment of vein via application of thermal energy.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The procedure to place the catheter within the vein is the same for radiofrequency ablation and endovenous laser ablation, also both procedures are conducted under duplex ultrasonography guidance (MSAC, 2011).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The physiological mechanism of vein ablation is also the same, with thermal energy producing endothelial and vein wall damage, denaturing and occluding the vein to close the vein, abolishing venous reflux and visible varicosities (MSAC, 2011).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: ECVA is performed with tumescent anesthesia (Markovic and Shortell, 2014).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Tumescent anesthesia allows physicians to use large volumes (500 ml) of dilute (0.1%) lidocaine in a single session while achieving anesthesia levels equivalent to those achieved with 1% lidocaine.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: In this way, the entire thigh portion of the GSV can be safely anesthetized (and consequently obliterated) at one time.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Epinephrine can be added to the solution to improve postoperative hemostasis, increase venous contraction around the heat-generating catheter, and lengthen the duration of postprocedural analgesia.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: A common formula for the tumescent anesthesia solution is 450 ml of normal saline mixed with 50 ml of 1% lidocaine with epinephrine (1:100,000 dilution) and 10 ml of sodium bicarbonate to buffer the acidity of the lidocaine.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Endovenous laser ablation of saphenous vein (ELAS) is a treatment alternative to surgical ligation and stripping of the greater saphenous vein.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Endovenous laser therapy for varicose veins is indicated for patients with clinically documented primary venous reflux, confirmed by duplex ultrasound, of the great or small saphenous veins (MSAC, 2008).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Endovenous laser ablation is only suitable for patients with large, saphenous varicose veins, as the catheter requires saphenous veins with a minimum 4.5mm in diameter.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: These patients have exhausted other conservative treatment measures and sclerotherapy is considered unlikely to be successful (MSAC, 2008).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: After ultrasound examination to confirm the site and extent of saphenous reflux, a catheter is introduced into the damaged vein along a guide wire via percutaneous puncture at the distal extent of the diseased saphenous vein (MSAC, 2008).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Perivascular infiltration of dilute local anesthetic along the length of the vein is then performed under ultrasound guidance to collapse the lumen and compress the vein onto the catheter, to dissipate heat generated during the procedure so as to prevent tissue damage, and to anesthetize the vein (MSAC).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The guide wire is replaced with a laser probe introduced through the catheter to just below the saphenofemoral or saphenopopliteal junction, with positioning confirmed by ultrasound.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Laser energy is then applied as the fiber and catheter are slowly withdrawn so as to close the vein and abolish venous reflux.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Pulses of laser light are emitted inside the vein, and the vein collapses, and seals shut.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: This procedure may be performed in the office under local anesthesia.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: A bandage or compression hose is placed on the treated leg following the treatment.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The procedure is performed on an outpatient basis.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Endovenous laser treatment can only be used for large veins, as a catheter must be inserted into the lumen of the vein to be treated (MSAC, 2008).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Endovenous laser treatment is not viable on saphenous veins smaller than 4.5 mm in diameter, and cannot be used for the treatment of small veins or telangiectases.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Smaller veins may be treated with sclerotherapy or ambulatory phlebectomy.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: A range of laser wavelengths can be used to achieve occlusion; there is no strong evidence to indicate that any particular wavelength is superior to any other (MSAC, 2008).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: One systematic evidence review reported that the short term (within 6 months) reported occlusion rates of the GSV and small saphenous vein (SSV) found in studies of endovenous laser therapy were all greater than 90%.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Absolute contraindications to ELAS treatment include occlusive deep venous thrombosis and pregnancy.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Relative contraindications include occlusive arterial disease, hypercoabulability, tortuous veins, and inability to ambulate (MSAC, 2008).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Endoluminal radiofrequency thermal heating (VNUS Closure Procedure) has been used with or without ligation and division for treatment of incompetence of the saphenofemoral and saphenopopliteal junction.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: To perform the radiofrequency ablation (RFA) procedure, the affected leg is prepared and draped, and a superficial local anaesthetic agent is used to anesthetize the site of cannulation.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: A radiofrequency catheter is inserted into the lumen of the greater saphenous vein, starting at its junction with the femoral vein.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Under some protocols, the placement of the catheter is guided by duplex ultrasonography.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The radiofrequency catheter heats the inner lumen of the vein to 85°C, with subsequent scarring and closure of the treated vein.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The procedure is performed in an office setting without general anesthesia; treatment time averages 20 mins.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Adverse sequelae include purpura, erythema and pain, which generally resolve days or weeks after treatment, and indurated fibrous cords that may remain for several months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Upon completion of the RFA procedure, the site of venous puncture is dressed, and compression stockings and/or bandages are applied as appropriate to reduce the risk of venous thromboembolism and to reduce postoperative bruising and tenderness (MSAC, 2011).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs are commonly used for post-procedural pain relief.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: For most patients additional procedures such as sclerotherapy or phlebectomy are required for the treatment of superficial veins below the knee, any tributary varicose veins, and telangiectases.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: These procedures may be performed during the RFA or endovenous laser treatment procedure, or over one or two follow-up visits.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Radiofrequency ablation is designed as a single-use therapeutic intervention, delivered as a single course of treatment per affected leg to obliterate the great or small saphenous veins through the application of thermal energy (MSAC, 2011).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: While generally indicated for primary varicose veins, re-treatment of varicose veins with RFA may be possible in some patients where neovascularisation or revascularisation has occurred.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: However, revascularization in the short term following treatment is uncommon.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Studies reporting on radiofrequency ablation with the more efficient second generation catheters report ablation rates close to 100% at 6-month follow-up with no major adverse events (MAS, 2011).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Prospective case series extending to 24 months have shown success rates with RFA similar to those reported for vein ligation and stripping.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Weiss and Weiss (2002) reported complete disappearance of the treated saphenous vein in 90 % of 21 patients followed for 24 months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Endothermal radiofrequency thermal heating may be performed with or without high ligation of the greater saphenous vein.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Chandler et al (2000) found no statistically significant difference in 1-year success rates from endovenous radiofrequency catheter ablation in 120 limbs treated without saphenofemoral ligation and 60 limbs treated with saphenofemoral ligation.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that "these early results suggest that extended sapheno-femoral junction (SFJ) ligation may add little to effective GSV [greater saphenous vein] obliteration, but our findings are not sufficiently robust to warrant abandonment of SFJ ligation as currently practiced in the management of primary varicose veins associated with GSV reflux."
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Pivotal studies of endovenous catheter ablation (endovenous laser ablation and endovenous radiofrequency ablation) procedures have focused on junctional incompetence.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: There is a lack of evidence of the effectiveness of endovenous catheter ablation procedures for treatment of varicose tributaries and perforator veins.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In addition, there are no studies comparing endovenous catheter ablation procedures to standard methods of treating varicose tributaries and perforator veins with sclerotherapy and ambulatory phlebectomy.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The Society for Interventional Radiologists (2003) has a position statement on VNUS that states that “(d)uplex ultrasound is necessary to map the anatomy of the venous system prior to the procedure, and imperative during the procedure for correct catheter placement and for proper tumescent anesthetic administration to minimize potential complications.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Duplex ultrasound also is necessary for follow-up after endovenous ablation.”
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Sadick (2000) has noted that the new less-invasive technologies for treatment of varicose veins must be evaluated with caution. "
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Long-term studies with other technologies must be compared with surgical ligation of the incompetent SFJ (sapheno-femoral junction).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Six-month and 5-year follow-ups are two different end- points.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The latter is a more accurate time interval of therapeutic efficacy."
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Subfascial endoscopic perforator vein surgery (SEPS) is a minimally invasive endoscopic procedure that eliminates the need for a large incision in the leg.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: It has been explored as an alternative to the traditional open surgical treatment of chronic venous insufficiency.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The aim of the procedure is to interrupt incompetent medial calf perforating veins to reduce venous reflux and decrease ambulatory venous hypertension in critical areas above the ankle where venous ulcers most frequently develop.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Kalra and Gloviczki (2002) stated that available evidence confirmed the superiority of SEPS over open perforator ligation, but do not address its role in the surgical treatment of advanced chronic venous insufficiency (CVI) and venous ulceration.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Ablation of superficial reflux by high ligation and stripping of the greater saphenous vein with avulsion of branch varicosities is concomitantly performed in the majority of patients undergoing SEPS.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The clinical and hemodynamic improvements attributable to SEPS thus are difficult to ascertain.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: As with open perforator ligation, clinical and hemodynamic results are better in patients with primary valvular incompetence (PVI) than in those with the post-thrombotic (PT) syndrome.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Until prospective, randomized, multicenter clinical studies are performed to address lingering questions regarding the effectiveness of SEPS, the procedure is recommended in patients with advanced CVI secondary to PVI of superficial and perforating veins, with or without deep venous incompetence.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The performance of SEPS in patients with PT syndrome remains controversial.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Contraindications for SEPS include associated arterial occlusive disease, infected ulcer, a non-ambulatory patient, and a medically high-risk patient.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Diabetes, renal failure, liver failure, morbid obesity, ulcers in patients with rheumatoid arthritis, or scleroderma, and presence of deep vein obstruction at the level of the popliteal vein or higher on pre-operative imaging are relative contraindications.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Patients with extensive skin changes, circumferential large ulcers, recent deep vein thrombosis, severe lymphedema, or large legs may not be suitable candidates (Kalra and Gloviczki, 2002).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: McDonagh et al (2002, 2003) has reported on the effectiveness of ultrasound-guided foam sclerotherapy (comprehensive objective mapping, precise image-guided injection, anti-reflux positioning and sequential sclerotherapy (COMPASS) technique) in the treatment persons with varicosities of the greater saphenous vein with saphenous vein reflux.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Published studies of the COMPASS technique involve relatively short-term follow up.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Study subjects were followed for 3 years, and for only 2 years after completion of a series of repeat sclerotherapy injections that were administered over 1 year.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In addition, these studies do not include a comparable group of subjects treated with surgery, which has been the primary method of treating incompetent long saphenous veins.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Thus, it is not possible to reach definitive conclusions about the durability of results of the COMPASS technique or its effectiveness compared with surgery for treatment of greater saphenous vein varicosities and saphenofemoral incompetence.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In addition, published studies of the COMPASS technique come from a single group of investigators.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In reviewing the study by McDonagh (2002), Allegra (2003) commented: “Surgical treatment has a long history with 5-20 year follow-ups being routine.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The 3 year follow-up in the present study is certainly not comparable ….
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This study does not answer questions raised against ultrasound guided sclerotherapy.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: It would be important to have the relevant aspects of this study duplicated, reproduced, and verified.”
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Published long-term randomized controlled clinical studies have demonstrated that surgery plus sclerotherapy is more effective than surgery alone for treatment of varicosities associated with incompetence of the saphenofemoral junction.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Belcaro et al (2003) reported on the results from the Venous Disease International Control (VEDICO) trial, the first long-term randomized controlled clinical trial of foam sclerotherapy.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The VEDICO trial involved 749 patients with varicose veins and saphenous vein incompetence who were randomly treated by six different approaches: standard sclerotherapy, high-dose sclerotherapy, surgical ligation, stab avulsion, foam sclerotherapy, and combined surgery (ligation or stab avulsion) and high dose sclerotherapy.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: At 10 years, the occurrence of new veins was 56 % for standard sclerotherapy, 51 % for foam sclerotherapy, 49 % for high-dose sclerotherapy, 41 % for stab avulsion, 38 % for ligation, and 27 % for combined surgery and sclerotherapy.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Belcaro et al (2000) reported on the results of a randomized controlled clinical study comparing ultrasound- guided sclerotherapy with surgery alone or surgery combined with sclerotherapy in 96 patients with varicose veins and superficial venous incompetence.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Although all approaches were reported to be effective in controlling the progression of venous incompetence, surgery appeared to be the most effective method on a long-term basis, and that surgery combined with sclerotherapy may be more effective than surgery alone.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: After 10 years follow-up, no incompetence of the saphenofemoral junction was observed in both groups assigned to surgery, compared to 18.8 % of limbs of subjects assigned to ultrasound-guided sclerotherapy.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Of limbs treated with ultrasound-guided sclerotherapy, 43.8 % of the distal venous systems were incompetent, compared to 36 % of limbs of subjects treated with surgery alone, and 16.1 % of limbs of subjects treated with surgery plus sclerotherapy.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The L'Agence Nationale d' Accreditation et d'Evaluation en Sante (l'ANAES) (Grange et al, 1998) conducted a systematic review of the literature on the indications of surgery for varicose veins of the legs.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Given the lack of good scientific evidence on the various treatments for primary varicose veins, the working group made recommendations based on professional agreement.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: They concluded that surgery is the treatment of choice for saphenous veins with reflux.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: An evidence review of surgical treatments for deep venous incompetence by the Alberta Heritage Foundation for Medical Research (Scott and Corabain, 2003) stated that "(s)clerotherapy is particularly effective in superficial venous incompetence when there is a large vein located in close proximity to the ulcer.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: However, surgery is indicated when there is substantial proximal incompetence in a saphenous vein."
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: A comprehensive evidence review of sclerotherapy for varicose veins conducted by the Alberta Heritage Foundation for Medical Research (2003) concluded that "the reviewed evidence does not adequately address the questions; which sclerosant is superior and which technique with or without ultrasound guidance is most efficacious … In recent years, new methods such as ES (endovascular sclerotherapy) and foam sclerotherapy (using ultrasound guidance) have been developed and proposed to improve the safety and efficacy of sclerotherapy for various types of varicose veins.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Evidence about these new techniques for treating patients with incompetence of the long saphenous vein is limited."
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The assessment concluded that although " (s)clerotherapy appears to be the treatment of choice for reticular varicosities, telangiectasia and other small, unsightly blood vessels … (t)he place of sclerotherapy as the first treatment for larger varicose veins (saphenous or non-saphenous) remains controversial."
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: There is a lack of reliable evidence that one type of sclerosant is significantly better than any other (Tisi 2007; Jia et al, 2006).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Jia and colleagues (2007) evaluated the safety and effectiveness of foam sclerotherapy for varicose veins.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that serious adverse events associated with foam sclerotherapy are rare.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: However, there is insufficient evidence to allow a meaningful comparison of the effectiveness of this treatment with that of other minimally invasive therapies or surgery.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Kendler and associates (2007) noted that "(r)ecently the use of foam sclerotherapy had a renaissance.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Several studies have documented the efficacy of foam sclerotherapy in selected patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The possibility of treating patients in an outpatient setting, with low costs and rapidly, makes foam sclerotherapy very attractive compared to invasive and minimally invasive methods.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: However long-term follow-ups in properly controlled randomized trials are needed before foam sclerotherapy can be recommended as a routine procedure".
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The FDA has approved Asclera (polidocanol) injection (BioForm Medical Inc., Franksville, WI) to close spider veins (tiny varicose veins less than 1 millimeter in diameter) and reticular veins (those that are 1 to 3 millimeters in diameter).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: As these small veins have not been demonstrated to cause symptoms, treatment of these small veins is considered cosmetic.
sentences.label: CLAIM
